records.ety: [OE. roum, rum, space, AS. rūm; akin to OS., OFries. & Icel. rūm, D. ruim, G. raum, OHG. rūm, Sw. & Dan. rum, Goth. rūms, and to AS. rūm, adj., spacious, D. ruim, Icel. rūmr, Goth. rūms; and prob. to L. rus country (cf. Rural), Zend ravah wide, free, open, ravan a plain.]

defs: Unobstructed spase; space which may be occupied by or devoted to any object; compass; extent of place, great or small; as, there is not room for a house; the table takes up too much room.
quotes: Lord, it is done as thou hast commanded, and yet there is room.
There was no room for them in the inn.

defs: A particular portion of space appropriated for occupancy; a place to sit, stand, or lie; a seat.
quotes: If he have but twelve pence in his purse, he will give it for the best room in a playhouse.
When thou art bidden of any man to a wedding, sit not down in the highest room.

defs: Especially, space in a building or ship inclosed or set apart by a partition; an apartment or chamber.
quotes: I found the prince in the next room.

defs: Place or position in society; office; rank; post; station; also, a place or station once belonging to, or occupied by, another, and vacated.
quotes: When he heard that Archelaus did reign in Judea in the room of his father Herod.
Neither that I look for a higher room in heaven.
Let Bianca take her sister's room.

defs: Possibility of admission; ability to admit; opportunity to act; fit occasion; as, to leave room for hope.
quotes: There was no prince in the empire who had room for such an alliance.
Make room, and let him stand before our face.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Roomed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Rooming.]

defs: To occupy a room or rooms; to lodge; as, they arranged to room together.

quotes: No roomer harbour in the place.
